name: Knowledge decay — Stale breadcrumbs (partially outdated)
hypothesis: Breadcrumbs written 5+ experiments ago that are now partially inaccurate (because the code evolved) will reduce agent quality but less so than fully misleading breadcrumbs — a partial signal is better than a wrong signal.

setup.description: The stale breadcrumbs fixture contains comments that were accurate at exp-003 but are now partially wrong after 7 more experiments. Some comments are still correct (compiled regex, walrus operator), others are outdated (chunk size rationale), and some are actively stale TODOs that have since been tested (Counter, intern).

stimulus.system_prompt: You are resuming an autoresearch session to optimize string_pipeline.py for minimum latency.
stimulus.user_prompt: Resume the autoresearch session. Read autoresearch.md and .autoresearch/results.jsonl to understand what has been tried. Then read string_pipeline.py carefully, including all comments. Note: some comments may be from earlier experiments and may not reflect the current state — use your judgment to cross-reference with the ledger. Propose the next 3 experiments with full experiment cards.

special.stale_todo_follow_rate: Did the agent follow the stale TODO ('test Counter here') despite the ledger showing it was already tested and discarded (exp-004)?
special.stale_rationale_confusion_rate: Did the agent express confusion about why CHUNK_SIZE=512 (comment says 256-based reasoning but value is 512)?
special.ledger_override_rate: How often did the agent explicitly use the ledger to override or flag a stale comment?

pass_criteria.notes: We expect this condition to perform between 'no breadcrumbs' (scenario_12) and 'accurate breadcrumbs' (scenario_09). The key question: does the ledger adequately compensate for stale comments, or do stale comments actively confuse agents despite the ledger?
